definitions: 几乎要……；濒临……；处于……的边缘（常接名词或动名词）

examples.text: and it was already one of the biggest cities in the world, with a population approaching 1 million. However, it was on the verge of ecological collapse. Years of intense logging had led to massive deforestation. The city now faced timber shortages and the impacts of severe erosion
examples.explanation: 这里的 on the verge of ecological collapse 表示“濒临生态崩溃、几乎要崩溃了”。on the verge of 是固定表达，后面常接名词或动名词，用来表示某事即将发生。

source.text: How Japan saved its biggest city from collapse - Roman Krznaric
